message:
chore(.beads/.gitignore): ignore .write.lock advisory flock

`.beads/.write.lock` is a zero-byte advisory flock file that `br`
touches on every open/close of the beads DB. It has no content and
no diagnostic value — it's only useful as a flock target while a
writer is holding it, and is expected to exist whenever the daemon
(or any short-lived CLI command) has touched the store. Showing up
as untracked on every agent session is pure noise.

Added under "Daemon runtime files" to sit next to the other
short-lived beads runtime files (`daemon.lock`, `daemon.log`,
`daemon.pid`, `bd.sock`).

files.patch:
@@ -11,6 +11,9 @@ daemon.log
 daemon.pid
 bd.sock
 
+# Beads writer advisory flock (touched on every br open/close, never useful in git)
+.write.lock
+
 # Sync runtime/backoff state (created/removed by bd sync)
 sync-state.json
